title: brassica oleracea var. communis
intro: Brassica oleracea var. communis, commonly known as cabbage, is a cool-season annual or biennial plant belonging to the Brassicaceae family, cultivated for its edible leaves and inflorescences. The medicinal properties of cabbage include its high content of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of consuming cabbage include its ability to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease, diabetes, and certain types of cancer, as well as its potential to support digestive health and immune function. Cabbage is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, soups, and tinctures, and can also be consumed raw in salads or fermented as sauerkraut.
